skill.name: happyhorse-1-0
skill.description: Generate text-to-video with HappyHorse 1.0 on RunComfy. Documents HappyHorse 1.0's strengths (#1 on Artificial Analysis Video Arena, native 1080p with in-pass synchronized audio, multi-shot character consistency, 6-language prompt support), the duration / aspect-ratio / resolution schema, and when to route to Wan 2.7 / Seedance 2 / LTX 2 instead. Calls `runcomfy run happyhorse/happyhorse-1-0/text-to-video` through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "happyhorse", "happy horse", "happyhorse 1.0", "happyhorse video", or any explicit ask to generate video with this model.
skill.summary: Text-to-video generation using HappyHorse 1.0 AI model with native 1080p output and synchronized audio generation.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate AI video generation skill for RunComfy's HappyHorse 1.0 model. The static analyzer flagged 86 potential issues, but evaluation reveals most are false positives from markdown code blocks being misidentified as executable code. The skill legitimately invokes the RunComfy CLI to call an external API for video generation. No malicious intent detected. Security documentation is comprehensive.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Command Invocation
medium_findings.description: The skill invokes the RunComfy CLI (`runcomfy run`) to execute video generation requests. While this is legitimate functionality for an AI video service, external command execution is a risk factor that warrants warning for users.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Pattern False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 66 'external_commands' patterns (Ruby/shell backtick execution) and 9 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flags, but these are all in markdown code blocks showing bash command examples. The SKILL.md file is documentation, not executable code. No actual shell execution occurs from this file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is a markdown documentation file containing bash command examples in code blocks (```bash ... ```). The 'backtick execution' detection is a false positive - these are not Ruby scripts with actual backtick operators, but documentation showing CLI command syntax.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Pattern False Positive
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged path traversal sequences at lines 180-182, but these are legitimate CLI operations: the CLI downloads generated video outputs and polls an API endpoint. These are expected filesystem operations for a video generation tool.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged 'path traversal' patterns are part of legitimate CLI workflow documented in lines 180-182: downloading video outputs from `.runcomfy.net` or `.runcomfy.com` URLs. This is not an attack but expected file download functionality.

content.value_statement: Create professional text-to-video content with HappyHorse 1.0, the top-ranked model on the Artificial Analysis Video Arena. Generate native 1080p videos with synchronized audio in a single pass.
actual_capabilities: Generate 1080p text-to-video content with HappyHorse 1.0 AI model
Produce synchronized audio (dialogue, ambient sound, Foley) in the same pass as video
Maintain character and wardrobe consistency across multiple shots
Support 6 prompt languages (Chinese, English, Japanese, Korean, German, French)
Create videos from 3 to 15 seconds duration
limitations: Maximum video duration is 15 seconds per generation
No support for image-to-video or reference video input on this endpoint
Audio cannot be driven by external audio files - in-pass audio only
Only 5 aspect ratios supported; ultra-wide cinematic formats get rejected

use_cases.title: Create Multi-Shot Brand Stories
use_cases.description: Generate consistent character appearances across multiple video segments for branded content campaigns. Ideal for advertisers who need a single character to appear consistently across a series of short-form videos.

use_cases.title: Produce Talking-Head Explainers
use_cases.description: Create educational or promotional videos with voiceover and ambient audio generated simultaneously with the video. The in-pass audio feature eliminates the need for separate audio production workflows.

use_cases.title: Generate Multilingual Short-Form Ads
use_cases.description: Produce platform-native vertical videos (9:16) with localized prompts in 6 supported languages. Perfect for social media campaigns targeting international audiences without script quality degradation.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and international marketing teams

prompt_templates.title: Basic Cinematic Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Wide shot. [subject description with motion]. [camera movement]. [lighting direction and quality]. [additional visual details].
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a quick cinematic video with straightforward subject action

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Shot Character Consistency
prompt_templates.prompt: Shot 1: [scene setup with character description including clothing, appearance details]. Shot 2: Same [character description from Shot 1], [new action or location with consistent appearance description].
prompt_templates.scenario: When creating a story with the same character across multiple shots

prompt_templates.title: Vertical Platform Content
prompt_templates.prompt: 9:16 vertical short. [subject action with visual details]. [camera style for vertical]. [audio direction with ambient or dialogue cues].
prompt_templates.scenario: When creating content optimized for TikTok, Instagram Reels, or YouTube Shorts

prompt_templates.title: Complex Multi-Element Scene
prompt_templates.prompt: [Shot type]. [Primary subject with specific appearance and clothing]. [Secondary element or environment]. [Temporal progression of action]. [Cinematic lens and lighting specs]. [Audio direction]
prompt_templates.scenario: When creating detailed narrative content with multiple visual elements

output_examples.input: A barista in a black apron pulls a single espresso shot, steam rising into the morning sun, rich crema slowly forming
output_examples.output: A 5-second 1080p vertical video showing a close-up handheld shot of the espresso extraction process with warm cafe ambience and the hiss of the steam wand

output_examples.input: Shot 1: Medium close-up. A woman in a navy trench coat enters a rain-slick neon-lit Tokyo alley. Shot 2: Same woman in same navy trench, now under the awning of a ramen shop
output_examples.output: A two-shot sequence with consistent character appearance: first shot shows her entering the alley under rain, second shows her under the awning with warm interior glow and gentle rain sounds

best_practices: Describe motion over time with temporal verbs rather than static descriptions. Say 'A woman turns from the window, walks two paces' instead of 'A woman drinking coffee'
Front-load camera and shot direction at the start of your prompt. Phrases like 'Wide shot.', 'Tracking shot.', or 'Locked tripod, low angle.' work as real directives
Restate character appearance anchors in each shot when generating multi-shot content. HappyHorse holds the look but needs the anchor description repeated
anti_patterns: Avoid static-frame descriptions with no temporal verbs - motion will be vague and unfocused
Do not pile up conflicting style directions in a single prompt - conflicting directions cancel each other out
Keep prompts under 2,500 characters - longer prompts cause quality degradation in generation

faq.question: What video resolution does HappyHorse 1.0 support?
faq.answer: HappyHorse 1.0 supports native 1080p output as well as 720p for faster, cheaper generations. Resolution is set via the 'resolution' parameter in the API call.

faq.question: How long can the generated videos be?
faq.answer: Video duration can be set from 3 to 15 seconds per generation. For longer narratives, you should segment into multiple multi-shot prompts and stitch the results together.

faq.question: Can I use external audio to drive lip-sync?
faq.answer: No, this text-to-video endpoint does not support external audio input. Audio is generated in-pass with the video. For audio-driven lip-sync, use Wan 2.7 or Seedance 2.0 Pro instead.

faq.question: What languages are supported for prompts?
faq.answer: Prompts can be written in 6 languages: Chinese, English, Japanese, Korean, German, and French. There is no script quality drop between languages.

faq.question: How do I maintain character consistency across multiple shots?
faq.answer: Include a full appearance anchor (clothing, colors, accessories) in each shot description. For example: 'Shot 1: A woman in a red wool coat, blue scarf. Shot 2: Same woman in red coat with blue scarf, now walking through a doorway.'

faq.question: What happens if I use an unsupported aspect ratio?
faq.answer: Unsupported aspect ratios will result in a 422 error. HappyHorse 1.0 only supports five ratios: 16:9, 9:16, 1:1, 4:3, and 3:4.
